input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on macOS, targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [https://dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)
- Download the latest **.NET 8 or .NET 9 SDK** (LTS recommended) for macOS (Arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and related workloads.
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

## 3. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [https://developer.apple.com/xcode/](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (requires an Apple ID).
- After installing, accept the license and install CLI tools:

```bash
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install
```

- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`
- Open Xcode → Settings → Platforms → ensure **iOS Simulator** runtime is downloaded.

## 4. Install Android SDK & JDK (for Android)

The easiest path — let MAUI handle it automatically on first build. Alternatively, install manually:

### Option A: Automatic (recommended)
Build any MAUI Android project once; the SDK auto-provisions to `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`.

### Option B: Manual
- **Android Studio:** [https://developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
  - Open SDK Manager → install: **Android SDK Platform 34+**, **Android SDK Build-Tools**, **Android SDK Command-line Tools**, **Android Emulator**, and a **system image** for the emulator.
- **JDK:** .NET MAUI bundles Microsoft's OpenJDK. If you need it separately: `dotnet workload install maui` includes it, or install via [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download).

Set environment variables (add to `~/.zshrc`):

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export JAVA_HOME="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/microsoft-17.jdk/Contents/Home"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$PATH"
```

**Verify:**
```bash
adb --version
emulator -list-avds
```

## 5. (Optional but recommended) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code + .NET MAUI extension** | [https://code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + install the **".NET MAUI" extension** from the marketplace |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) — has built-in MAUI support |

> **Note:** Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code or Rider are the recommended IDEs on macOS.

## 6. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<simulator-udid>

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

Or use the one-stop diagnostic check:

```bash
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
xcodebuild -version
adb devices
```

## 7. Discover the Right Versions

| Component | How to check latest |
|-----------|-------------------|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet sdk check` or [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Xcode | App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode/releases](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/releases/) |
| Android API levels | Android Studio SDK Manager or [developer.android.com/studio/releases/platforms](https://developer.android.com/studio/releases/platforms) |

## TL;DR Install Order

1. **.NET SDK** → 2. `dotnet workload install maui` → 3. **Xcode** (+ accept license + simulator) → 4. **Android SDK** (auto or via Android Studio) → 5. **IDE** (VS Code + extension or Rider) → 6. `dotnet new maui` and build to verify.
